# Document with Links

This document demonstrates various link types and reference patterns in markdown.

## External Links

Visit [GitHub](https://github.com) for code hosting, or check out [MDN Web Docs](https://developer.mozilla.org) for web development resources.

You can also use reference-style links: [Google][1] and [Wikipedia][2] are popular websites.

[1]: https://www.google.com
[2]: https://www.wikipedia.org

## Relative Links

See the [installation guide](./installation.md) for setup instructions, or check the [API reference](../api/README.md) for technical details.

## Email and Auto-links

Contact us at <support@example.com> or visit <https://example.com> for more information.

## Anchor Links

Jump to [External Links](#external-links) or [Relative Links](#relative-links) sections above.

## Image Links

![Alt text](https://via.placeholder.com/150 "Placeholder image")

## Nested Links

This is a [link with **bold** text](https://example.com) and another with [inline `code`](https://example.com/code).

## Long URL

Visit https://www.example.com/very/long/path/to/resource?param1=value1&param2=value2&param3=value3 for detailed information about this specific resource with many query parameters.

## Conclusion

Links are fundamental to markdown and enable navigation between documents and external resources.
